questions.question: Explain the difference between a NAND gate and a NOR gate with truth tables.
keyPoints: NAND produces 0 only when all inputs are 1
NOR produces 1 only when all inputs are 0
NAND is AND followed by NOT
NOR is OR followed by NOT
Show complete 2-input truth tables for both

questions.question: Why are NAND and NOR gates called universal gates? Explain with an example.
keyPoints: Universal gates can implement any Boolean function
NAND alone can create NOT, AND, OR, XOR
Example: NOT gate using NAND (connect inputs together)
Any combinational circuit can be built using only NAND or only NOR

questions.question: Draw the logic symbols for AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR, and XNOR gates.
keyPoints: AND: Flat front with curved output
OR: Curved front with curved output
NOT: Triangle with bubble at output
NAND: AND with bubble
NOR: OR with bubble
XOR: OR with extra curved line
XNOR: XOR with bubble

questions.question: What is the XOR gate? Explain its operation with a truth table and one practical application.
keyPoints: XOR outputs 1 when inputs are different
XOR outputs 0 when inputs are same
Truth table showing all 4 input combinations
Practical use: Parity checking, addition in binary, comparison

questions.question: State and explain De Morgan's theorems with Boolean expressions and logic gate implementations.
keyPoints: First theorem: (A+B)' = A' · B'
Second theorem: (A·B)' = A' + B'
Show how OR with inverted inputs equals AND
Show how AND with inverted inputs equals OR

questions.question: Explain fan-in and fan-out in digital logic gates.
keyPoints: Fan-in: maximum inputs a gate can handle
Fan-out: number of gate inputs a single output can drive
Affects circuit design and performance
High fan-out may cause signal degradation

questions.question: What is propagation delay in logic gates? Why is it important in digital circuit design?
keyPoints: Time between input change and output change
Determines maximum operating frequency
Affects timing in sequential circuits
Can cause race conditions if not accounted for

questions.question: Design a circuit that produces output 1 when majority of its three inputs are 1. Implement using basic gates and explain.
keyPoints: Majority function: output 1 when 2 or more inputs are 1
Truth table with 8 combinations
Expression: Y = AB + BC + AC
Implementation using AND and OR gates
Can also implement using NAND gates only

questions.question: Implement the Boolean function F = A + B'C using only NAND gates. Show step-by-step simplification and the circuit diagram.
keyPoints: Convert expression to NAND form
Use NAND as universal gate
Step-by-step transformation
Final circuit with only NAND gates
Verify using truth table

questions.question: Design a 2-to-1 multiplexer using basic logic gates. Explain its operation and give truth table.
keyPoints: Multiplexer selects one of two inputs based on select line
Use AND and OR gates with select line
Truth table showing input selection
Applications in data routing

questions.question: Explain bubble pushing technique for simplifying digital circuits. Provide an example with before and after circuit.
keyPoints: Bubbles represent inversion
Push bubbles through gates to simplify
Apply De Morgan's theorems
Example showing transformation
Benefits: reduced gate count
